items.headline: City addresses affordable housing protections when subsidies expire
items.summary: Council is reviewing policies to maintain affordability restrictions on subsidized housing even if project subsidies end or foreclosure occurs. This is a policy move to prevent affordable units from converting to market-rate housing.

items.headline: Comm Hill Phase 3.1 housing development approved on Altino Boulevard
items.summary: Four new homes permitted in the Comm Hill Phase 3.1 development along Altino Boulevard (lots 19-23), ranging from $487k-$573k. This represents a notable cluster of single-family home construction in what appears to be a larger planned community development.

items.headline: City Council reviews FY 2026-27 Capital Improvement Program
items.summary: Mountain View leaders are studying the capital projects planned for the next fiscal year, which typically includes infrastructure, facilities, and major city investments. This is worth tracking if you're interested in what physical improvements or development projects the city is prioritizing.

items.headline: Sunnyvale considers changes to tenant protection rules
items.summary: The City Council is introducing an ordinance to amend the Residential Tenant Protections Program, which could affect rental housing standards and tenant rights in Sunnyvale. Details on what specific protections are being added or modified were not provided in the agenda.

items.headline: City releases Children and Youth Services Master Plan status
items.summary: San José is updating its comprehensive plan for youth programs and services. The report includes updates on youth empowerment initiatives and the Safe Summer Initiative, signaling the city's direction for youth-focused investments.

items.headline: Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan moving forward
items.summary: The city is developing a plan to improve library facilities and user experience across San José's system. This suggests potential changes or upgrades to branch locations and services residents use.

items.headline: Downtown building gets historic status upgrade and density bonus
items.summary: The City Council considered elevating 405 Kipling Street from Category 4 to Category 2 historic status, which would allow the developer to add 5,000 square feet of bonus floor area in exchange for seismic and historic rehabilitation work. This is a notable downtown development that increases building capacity while preserving historic character.
